whatThisIs: Reference the modified Rankin Scale (mRS), the seven-grade scale from 0 to 6 used to document global disability after stroke. mRS 0 is no symptoms; 1 is no significant disability despite some symptoms; 2 is slight disability but the patient handles their own affairs without assistance; 3 is moderate disability requiring some help; 4 is moderately severe disability and inability to walk unaided; 5 is severe disability with bed-bound or constant nursing care; 6 is death. Sophie Well lists each grade with the standard one-line definition.
whenToUse: Use this when documenting a 90-day or discharge functional outcome after acute stroke, when reviewing a trial endpoint that defines a 'good outcome' as mRS 0-2, or when an inpatient stroke team needs to communicate disability level to a rehab facility or family in a consistent shorthand. The scale is observer-reported and has known interrater variability; structured interviews (mRS-9Q, simplified mRS questionnaire) tighten reliability but the grades themselves are unchanged.
